summary: A rectangular equestrian arena or stable structure with a gable roof. The building features brick and stone walls with horizontal striping, white concrete pillar supports at corners, and a dark wood/dark oak roof. The interior is open with a dirt floor arena. The structure is symmetrical with two distinct roof peaks visible from aerial views.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with gable roof peaks
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along length and width axes

rebuild_hints: Build rectangular frame 16 blocks wide × 24 blocks deep
Construct walls with alternating brick and stonebrick in horizontal stripes
Place white concrete pillars at all four corners for structural support
Create gable roof using dark oak/dark wood blocks with two distinct peaks
Leave interior completely open (no floor blocks, just dirt)
Add white concrete block perimeter fence around base exterior
Use stone slabs for roof edge detailing
uncertainties: Exact height of roof peaks (estimated 8 blocks total, but could be 7-9)
Precise brick-to-stone ratio in wall striping pattern
Whether interior has any internal support beams (not visible from exterior)
Exact depth dimension (estimated 24, could be 20-28 based on perspective)
Type of wood used for roof (dark oak vs dark wood vs spruce)
